title: Why would someone place a drawing of Sephiroths tree in the ceiling of his office
display_title: Why would someone place a drawing of Sephiroths tree in the ceiling of his office
wikitext:
[]: Why would someone place a drawing of Sephiroth's tree in the ceiling of his office?             Quote Post by The Flying Fortress » Mon Jul 03, 2017 8:17 pm Looking at the meaning of this to his plan, how can Gendo make it so visible? How come no one noticed that strange decoration on the ceiling and did not try to connect the dots?

[]: Re: Why would someone place a drawing of Sephiroth's tree in the ceiling of his office?             Quote Post by C.T.1290 » Mon Jul 03, 2017 8:50 pm Maybe Gendo just like the style, as if it gives him an air of superiority in his office. It could also be an indication of how he and SEELE are devoted to their plans of the he Human Instrumentality Project. And the tree of Sephiroth has a significance as it was shown in the End of Evangelion.

[]: Re: Why would someone place a drawing of Sephiroth's tree in the ceiling of his office?             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Jul 03, 2017 10:59 pm Who else is going to have any idea what it means? To an outside observer, Gendo might just be pretentious.

[]: Re: Why would someone place a drawing of Sephiroth's tree in the ceiling of his office?             Quote Post by Cybermat47 » Mon Jul 03, 2017 11:18 pm Sachi wrote: Who else is going to have any idea what it means?

[]: Re: Why would someone place a drawing of Sephiroth's tree in the ceiling of his office?             Quote Post by Settie » Mon Jul 03, 2017 11:40 pm Basically what Sachi said, if someone had a poster of sugar water as its chemical formula in their office, how many people would actually know what it means? They'd probably just assume some sciency stuff and leave it at that.

[]: Re: Why would someone place a drawing of Sephiroth's tree in the ceiling of his office?             Quote Post by The Flying Fortress » Sat Jul 08, 2017 2:07 pm You basically want to tell me, '' Who cares about this, it's just an anime, '' but they do not find a polite way to talk

[]: Re: Why would someone place a drawing of Sephiroth's tree in the ceiling of his office?             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Jul 08, 2017 2:39 pm ^What?

[]: Re: Why would someone place a drawing of Sephiroth's tree in the ceiling of his office?             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Jul 08, 2017 2:45 pm Does anyone but Fuyutsuki even go in his office? Is it actually an office? His desk is suspiciously clean. What if it's a private brooding chamber and no one sees it but Gendo's sassy, rotund Swiss maid Helga, who does not appear in the series but is absolutely canon?

[]: Re: Why would someone place a drawing of Sephiroth's tree in the ceiling of his office?             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Jul 08, 2017 5:38 pm Kaji makes his delivery to Gendo's office. With that in mind, the one person besides Gendo and Fuyu to enter the office actually did end up being suspicious of Gendo and Seele's plans, and he died for it. Checkmate, OP.

[]: Re: Why would someone place a drawing of Sephiroth's tree in the ceiling of his office?             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Jul 08, 2017 5:41 pm It's an office, and, at least according to NGE2, he actually does paperwork there sometimes. Also, you guys are forgetting the time that Shinji is brought there in triple handcuffs.

[]: Re: Why would someone place a drawing of Sephiroth's tree in the ceiling of his office?             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sat Jul 08, 2017 5:53 pm Classic CEO Power play. NERV CONSTRUCTION: Plans are complete for your office, Mr. Ikari. It's a very (sigh) very large open space. Like excessively large. Like we had to create a whole new wing to the main offices just to fit it in. Before construction starts is there anything else you'd like to add? GENDO: The Tree of Sephiroth shall be painted on the ceiling. NERV CONSTRUCTION: What? GENDO: The Tree of Sephiroth shall be painted on the ceiling. NERV CONSTRUCTION: What's the Tree of Saphyroth? GENDO: It is not my job to educate you. One must educate themselves to be a full member of the human race. I repeat one final time, the Tree of Sephiroth shall be painted on the ceiling. Do it. NERV CONSTRUCTION: Um... yeah.... sure. Whatever you say. GENDO (VOICEOVER): Holy shit!!! They're actually gonna fucking doing it! A'ight, G-Man. Simmer down now. Be cool. Do the thing! Gendo Ikari leans over into the Gendo pose & smiles. GENDO (Ominous): It's all going according to plan.

[]: Re: Why would someone place a drawing of Sephiroth's tree in the ceiling of his office?             Quote Post by The Flying Fortress » Sat Jul 08, 2017 6:01 pm Sachi wrote: ^What?

[]: Re: Why would someone place a drawing of Sephiroth's tree in the ceiling of his office?             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Jul 08, 2017 6:15 pm Reichu wrote: It's an office,

[]: Re: Why would someone place a drawing of Sephiroth's tree in the ceiling of his office?             Quote Post by Cybermat47 » Sat Jul 08, 2017 6:15 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: Classic CEO Power play. NERV CONSTRUCTION: Plans are complete for your office, Mr. Ikari. It's a very (sigh) very large open space. Like excessively large. Like we had to create a whole new wing to the main offices just to fit it in. Before construction starts is there anything else you'd like to add? GENDO: The Tree of Sephiroth shall be painted on the ceiling. NERV CONSTRUCTION: What? GENDO: The Tree of Sephiroth shall be painted on the ceiling. NERV CONSTRUCTION: What's the Tree of Saphyroth? GENDO: It is not my job to educate you. One must educate themselves to be a full member of the human race. I repeat one final time, the Tree of Sephiroth shall be painted on the ceiling. Do it. NERV CONSTRUCTION: Um... yeah.... sure. Whatever you say. GENDO (VOICEOVER): Holy shit!!! They're actually gonna fucking doing it! A'ight, G-Man. Simmer down now. Be cool. Do the thing! Gendo Ikari leans over into the Gendo pose & smiles. GENDO (Ominous): It's all going according to plan.

[]: Re: Why would someone place a drawing of Sephiroth's tree in the ceiling of his office?             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Jul 08, 2017 6:20 pm He doesn't need the space on a practical level. For purposes of storytelling and reflecting his character through visuals, it fits. Empty, barren, shadowy, unwelcoming, singly devoted to one thing. The silhouette of the desk is looming and monolithic, used to effect during his confrontation with a detained Shinji. The symbols only reflect the mystery of his character initially, and it is not until later do we learn the connection between the symbols and Instrumentality, after we've learned more about Gendo's goals. Nobody picks up on the literal meaning of the symbols, because figuratively they're meant to represent mystery; that's the real reason they're there. No practical, in-story reason for any of it, just LOL SYMBOLISM like the religious stuff. Good symbolism, but LOLOLOL nonetheless.

[]: Re: Why would someone place a drawing of Sephiroth's tree in the ceiling of his office?             Quote Post by C.T.1290 » Sat Jul 08, 2017 6:22 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: Classic CEO Power play. NERV CONSTRUCTION: Plans are complete for your office, Mr. Ikari. It's a very (sigh) very large open space. Like excessively large. Like we had to create a whole new wing to the main offices just to fit it in. Before construction starts is there anything else you'd like to add? GENDO: The Tree of Sephiroth shall be painted on the ceiling. NERV CONSTRUCTION: What? GENDO: The Tree of Sephiroth shall be painted on the ceiling. NERV CONSTRUCTION: What's the Tree of Saphyroth? GENDO: It is not my job to educate you. One must educate themselves to be a full member of the human race. I repeat one final time, the Tree of Sephiroth shall be painted on the ceiling. Do it. NERV CONSTRUCTION: Um... yeah.... sure. Whatever you say. GENDO (VOICEOVER): Holy shit!!! They're actually gonna fucking doing it! A'ight, G-Man. Simmer down now. Be cool. Do the thing! Gendo Ikari leans over into the Gendo pose & smiles. GENDO (Ominous): It's all going according to plan.

[]: Re: Why would someone place a drawing of Sephiroth's tree in the ceiling of his office?             Quote Post by The Flying Fortress » Sat Jul 08, 2017 6:54 pm Sachi wrote: He doesn't need the space on a practical level. For purposes of storytelling and reflecting his character through visuals, it fits. Empty, barren, shadowy, unwelcoming, singly devoted to one thing. The silhouette of the desk is looming and monolithic, used to effect during his confrontation with a detained Shinji. The symbols only reflect the mystery of his character initially, and it is not until later do we learn the connection between the symbols and Instrumentality, after we've learned more about Gendo's goals. Nobody picks up on the literal meaning of the symbols, because figuratively they're meant to represent mystery; that's the real reason they're there. No practical, in-story reason for any of it, just LOL SYMBOLISM like the religious stuff. Good symbolism, but LOLOLOL nonetheless.

[]: Re: Why would someone place a drawing of Sephiroth's tree in the ceiling of his office?             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Jul 08, 2017 7:27 pm They're not even really flaws in logic, and symbolism isn't even really an "excuse". NGE is a work of fiction, and things need to serve the plot and character work before they need to serve practical in-universe purpose. Given that this is animation also, they should be making full use of visual storytelling, and that is something Anno has always excelled at. He's telling an allegory first and foremost, and everything from the giant aliens to the apocalyptic religious themes are serving that purpose. Why does Gendo have these symbols in his office? In-universe reason: because he wanted them. (No further explanation required, really.) Narrative reason: everything I stated in my earlier post. Which reason is more relevant to the point of the series?

[]: Re: Why would someone place a drawing of Sephiroth's tree in the ceiling of his office?             Quote Post by The Flying Fortress » Sat Jul 08, 2017 9:15 pm Sachi wrote: They're not even really flaws in logic, and symbolism isn't even really an "excuse". NGE is a work of fiction, and things need to serve the plot and character work before they need to serve practical in-universe purpose. Given that this is animation also, they should be making full use of visual storytelling, and that is something Anno has always excelled at. He's telling an allegory first and foremost, and everything from the giant aliens to the apocalyptic religious themes are serving that purpose. Why does Gendo have these symbols in his office? In-universe reason: because he wanted them. (No further explanation required, really.) Narrative reason: everything I stated in my earlier post. Which reason is more relevant to the point of the series?

[]: Re: Why would someone place a drawing of Sephiroth's tree in the ceiling of his office?             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Jul 08, 2017 11:14 pm Chuckman wrote: That really depends on how you define office and contemplation chamber.
